channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: China’s Expanding Interests in Latin America: Development, Leverage, Coercion, and Crime
item.description: To counter China’s port strategy in Latin America and the Caribbean, the United States must treat infrastructure as strategic competition. Beijing’s investments lock in influence, shape supply chains, and create economic leverage—demanding a stronger U.S. response.

item.title: Data Is Now the Front Line of Warfare
item.description: From Ukraine to the Gulf, private U.S. companies like Microsoft and AWS are now on the front lines of war. As Iran targets data centers, the Trump administration must decide how to protect them.

item.title: What Is Israel Trying to Accomplish in Lebanon?
item.description: Even as Israel and the United States fight in Lebanon, Israel is also in a major war with Hezbollah—a war designed to keep the group weak and off balance.

item.title: Demystifying Iranian Cyber Operations in the U.S.-Iran Conflict
item.description: Senior fellow Nikita Shah analyzes why Iran’s cyber operations will provide an incremental, rather than a decisive military edge in its conflict with the United States and Israel.

item.title: What Are the Implications of the Iran Conflict for Japan?
item.description: The Iran conflict will have both direct and indirect effects on Japan’s economic, security, and energy outlook, as well as its geopolitical environment.
